news.headline: 'You are Canadian heroes' - the forgotten hosts making World Cup history
news.desc: Canada are making history at this World Cup with a run that is likely to change the face of football in the country forever.

news.headline: Clarke leaves strong Scotland legacy but exit brings sense of relief
news.desc: Steve Clarke took Scotland out of the wilderness - but not past the group stage of a tournament. Tom English's must-read analysis of his reign.

news.headline: How Bellingham became Tuchel's most important player
news.desc: His starting place was the subject of much debate in Thomas Tuchel's England team, but Jude Bellingham has become integral to the manager's style of play.

news.headline: Knockouts are here - what is England's path to the final?
news.desc: BBC Sport takes a look at what England will need to do to reach the final after qualifying for the knockout stages.

news.headline: France the team to beat? Chris Sutton predicts World Cup's last-32 matches
news.desc: BBC Sport's football expert Chris Sutton gives his predictions for the last-32 ties at the 2026 World Cup, including England against DR Congo.

news.headline: 27 flights, 24 matches: The carbon cost of Fifa president's World Cup tour
news.desc: Gianni Infantino has clocked up thousands of air miles across North America so far.

news.headline: Conspiracy or chaos? Algeria & Austria defend 'unexpected' draw
news.desc: The 'Disgrace of Gijon' - an infamous match at the 1982 World Cup in Spain - has followed Austria and Algeria for 44 years. Did history repeat itself in Algeria's 3-3 draw with Austria on Sunday?

news.headline: Ugarte has 'most serious injury footballer can face'
news.desc: Manchester United have confirmed that midfielder Manuel Ugarte suffered knee ligament damage during Uruguay's World Cup defeat by Spain.

news.headline: What you need to know about England's opponents DR Congo
news.desc: England will face DR Congo in the round of 32. Here is what you need to know about them.

news.headline: 'Bye-bye, Scotland' - Clarke's leaving letter to Tartan Army
news.desc: Steve Clarke's letter to the Scotland fans in full following his departure as head coach.

news.headline: World Cup goals chart led by PSG and Real - plus Palace and Sunderland
news.desc: Crystal Palace and Sunderland lead the Premier League’s World Cup scoring charts after the 2026 group stage, ahead of Arsenal, Liverpool and Manchester City.

news.headline: Derided by Leeds, snubbed by USA - but Marsch becoming Canada hero
news.desc: Derided by Leeds fans and snubbed by the USA, Jesse Marsch has faced difficult moments in his career - but appears to have found his home with Canada as they look to reach the World Cup last 16.
